page: Microsoft Azure E4-2s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and large-block memory bandwidth tests all register in the poor category. This performance bottleneck is also reflected in weak Passmark CPU scores and poor database operations, indicating that the instance is constrained by its dual-vCPU configuration for parallelized workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4-2s_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. It is suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications with low CPU concurrency requirements. It is less suited for compute-heavy tasks, multi-threaded compilation, or high-throughput web serving due to its constrained multi-core performance.
description: The Microsoft Azure E4-2s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv5 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 16.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPU resources but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, though multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the poor to weak categories. Consequently, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-concurrency workloads. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching systems, and lightweight memory-intensive applications rather than parallelized compute tasks.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine delivering high memory density per core to support low-concurrency, memory-intensive workloads and caching applications.
